summary: Small unmerged THPDec matching/fix PR touching Dolphin THP decoder source and header layout. It renamed/typed several formerly padding-backed fields, adjusted THPInit toward the SDK behavior, changed __THPDecompressiMCURow512x448 to take an explicit THPFileInfo* instead of repeatedly using __THPInfo, and made small formatting/control-flow changes that affected codegen. The decomp.dev bot reported a net +984 matched bytes and one new full match for __THPHuffDecodeDCTCompY, but also reported a broken match in __THPPrepBitStream and a small regression in __THPReadScaneHeader; the PR was closed without merge.

key_files.role: Adjusted THP decoder structs and prototypes: THPHuffmanTab, THPComponent, THPFileInfo, THPInit, and __THPDecompressiMCURow512x448.
Updated THPDec implementation for explicit THPFileInfo use in the 512x448 iMCU path, THPInit low-cache/fast-cast setup, and several matching-sensitive style/codegen edits.

what_changed: THPHuffmanTab changed from a two-word pad2 array to a single pad2 word plus a named u16 num_Vij field; __THPReadHuffmanTableSpecification now writes info->huffmanTabs[tab_index].num_Vij instead of pad2[1].
THPComponent gained a leading u8 placeholder field named _, indicating a struct-layout correction before predDC.
THPFileInfo pad2 changed from a u32 array sized by division to a byte array sized as 0x83d - 0x756, preserving byte-accurate layout rather than implying word alignment.
__THPDecompressiMCURow512x448 prototype and definition changed from void(void) to void(THPFileInfo*), and __THPDecompressYUV now calls it with __THPInfo.

Inside __THPDecompressiMCURow512x448, many references to the global __THPInfo were replaced by the local info parameter for MCUsPerRow, component pointers, quant tables, restart interval state, bit count, and predDC reset.
THPInit changed from returning BOOL to returning void in the header and source.
THPInit gained hardware/cache setup logic: checks PPCMfhid2() & 0x10000000, conditionally calls DCInvalidateRange on 0xE0000000 for 0x4000 bytes, calls LCEnable(), and calls OSInitFastCast().
THPInit work-buffer offsets for __THPLCWork672 changed from base += 0x2A00 and base += 0xA80 to base += 0x2800 and base += 0xA00 before the final 0xA80 increment.
THPInit no longer sets __THPInitFlag = TRUE or returns TRUE in the diff excerpt.
Several brace/formatting changes were made around else blocks and a for loop, likely because they affected compiler output in this file.
smart_moves: Replacing pad2[1] with a named num_Vij field documents a real Huffman-table count without changing all surrounding logic, making later THP work easier to audit.
Using u8 pad2[0x83d - 0x756] in THPFileInfo is a byte-accurate way to represent an odd-sized gap; this avoids misleading u32 alignment assumptions in a binary-matching struct.

Passing THPFileInfo* explicitly into __THPDecompressiMCURow512x448 mirrors the existing helper style used by Huffman decode functions and can change register allocation/codegen compared with repeated global loads.
Restoring SDK-like initialization behavior in THPInit, including LCEnable and OSInitFastCast, moved THPInit substantially closer according to the bot report (+111 bytes, 10.60% to 32.12%).
The PR kept the change small and localized to THPDec.c and thp.h, making the matching impacts easy to isolate in the bot report.
decomp_lessons: For external SDK code, apparent padding often hides partially-known fields; once usage is known, name the field rather than indexing into a pad array.
Odd-byte struct gaps should be represented with byte arrays, not u32 arrays, when the offset arithmetic is not word-aligned.
Changing a helper from global-state access to an explicit context parameter can be a valid matching tactic, especially in functions already passing that context to nested helpers.
Small formatting changes such as brace placement can affect generated code in this codebase; preserve them when the diff is matching-driven rather than style-driven.

Bot reports should be checked for both new matches and regressions. This PR improved many THPDec items but broke __THPPrepBitStream from 100.00% to 99.96%, which is important evidence because the PR was not merged.
When changing SDK function signatures, update both header and source together; here THPInit and __THPDecompressiMCURow512x448 were changed in thp.h and THPDec.c.
naming_conventions: Use Dolphin/THP SDK-style prefixes: THP for public symbols and __THP for internal static helpers/globals.
Preserve existing camel/mixed-case SDK names such as THPVideoDecode, THPInit, __THPReadHuffmanTableSpecification, __THPDecompressiMCURow512x448, and __THPHuffDecodeDCTCompY.
Name recovered struct fields according to observed source semantics, e.g. num_Vij for the Huffman Vij count.
Use explicit placeholder/padding names only when semantics are still unknown, e.g. u8 _ and pad/pad2 fields.
Use dimension-specific helper suffixes for video paths, e.g. 512x448, 640x480, and NxN.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Explicit THPFileInfo* parameter in __THPDecompressiMCURow512x448 replaced repeated __THPInfo global accesses, likely influencing register allocation and load patterns.
The byte-array THPFileInfo pad preserves exact offsets around dLC and components, which is critical for matching code that accesses later fields.
Changing THPHuffmanTab layout to expose num_Vij avoids writing through pad2[1] and can alter access size/offset in __THPReadHuffmanTableSpecification.
THPInit matching was improved by adding low-cache initialization calls and OSInitFastCast instead of leaving SDK calls commented out.
Brace placement edits around else blocks and the for loop in Huffman decode code were included in the diff and should be treated as potentially codegen-relevant for this compiler setup.
decomp.dev reported __THPHuffDecodeDCTCompY became a full match at 100.00%, while THPInit improved by +111 bytes but was still only 32.12% matched.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
decomp.dev bot report for GALE01 showed matched code increased to 58.01% (+0.03%, +984 bytes).
Bot reported 1 new match: main/dolphin/thp/THPDec __THPHuffDecodeDCTCompY improved from 99.99% to 100.00%.
Bot reported 1 broken match: __THPPrepBitStream dropped from 100.00% to 99.96%.
Bot reported improvements in THPInit, __THPReadHuffmanTableSpecification, __THPHuffDecodeDCTCompV, __THPDecompressiMCURow640x480, __THPDecompressiMCURowNxN, and __THPHuffDecodeDCTCompU.
Bot reported one unmatched-item regression: __THPReadScaneHeader changed from 42.59% to 42.57%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching THPInit to see whether the void signature, LCEnable setup, OSInitFastCast call, or __THPInitFlag removal was retained.
Find later PRs fixing the __THPPrepBitStream regression introduced or exposed by this PR.
Search for num_Vij in THPHuffmanTab to confirm whether the field name/layout became canonical.
Compare later THPDec.c versions for whether __THPDecompressiMCURow512x448 keeps the THPFileInfo* parameter.
Look for later struct-layout corrections around THPFileInfo pad2 and THPComponent leading placeholder byte.
